slide_1_Cover: DOPDA: AI Objective Insurance Recommendation Platform
slide_2_Idea_Problem_Solution_Value: Zero GA or insurer affiliation means recommendations based purely on customer value not commission structure — structural independence no GA-tied competitor can replicate
74% of users willing to pay ~$7 for AI analysis; agents see 2x conversion on pre-qualified leads vs traditional DB; 38.3% proposal-to-consultation conversion proven
CEO and CPO bring 20yr combined insurance expertise from Heungkuk plus CTO AI engineering from DayTrip — rare domain plus technology founding team with $65K invested
AI Design Manager scans all products across 30+ insurers in real-time and generates a preliminary insurance design optimized for customer value with zero commission bias
Full-cycle post-purchase support including policy reviews, claims filing, and coverage updates as life changes — solving the orphan policy problem after agents leave
Agents receive only customers who reviewed an AI-designed proposal and actively requested consultation — pre-qualified high-intent leads with higher conversion
Agents recommend high-commission products over customer interest — consumers cannot verify if advice is genuinely for them or just profitable for the agent
Agents waste hours on unqualified leads from expensive DB purchases and cold-calling while designing proposals without knowing if they are objectively the best option
No independent platform compares products across all 30+ Korean insurers using AI and pre-qualifies customers before connecting them to the right agents
slide_3_Market_1_3_TAM_SAM_SOM_+_Why_Now: Korea insurance market stays 70%+ agent-driven with consumer distrust of agent objectivity — ideal proving ground for AI-powered conflict-free insurance design
FSC 2026 commission reform shifts agent incentives from upfront sales to long-term maintenance — DB lead market collapsing as agents need new pre-qualified lead sources
Japan $325B insurance market mirrors GA bias with 92.9% face-to-face sales through 200K agents; SEA agent-dependent markets growing with identical structural pain
Total insurance premiums written in Korea (2024)
New business premiums in protection-type insurance (2024)
High-intent 30s–40s consumers + GA & independent agents seeking AI-qualified leads
Non-life long-term personal insurance new business: $5.91B
Consumers: 30s–40s actively seeking neutral, AI-driven guidance to compare insurance proposals
Agents: GA and independent agents needing high-intent, chat-ready leads to replace cold DB outreach
slide_4_Market_2_3_Competition: Insurance analysis app with owned GA subsidiary — 2023 revenue $11M with strongest domestic consumer and agent coverage
AI insurance analysis platform with 2M+ data points and 2.3M downloads — IPO-bound with proven commercial value in recommendation
Platform-powered GA surpassing 2,000 agents with first profitability — leverages Toss ecosystem for distribution at scale
India largest online insurance marketplace at $7.9B market cap — proves comparison platforms can reach public-market scale
AI Design Manager pre-designs objective plans before any agent involvement — eliminates structural commission bias at root rather than layering comparison on top of GA
Zero GA affiliation means truly independent recommendations across all 30+ insurers — every competitor is tied to their own agency revenue structure creating inherent bias
Two-sided value creation where consumers get AI-designed objective plans and agents get pre-qualified high-intent leads with 2x conversion — preferred platform for both sides
slide_5_Market_3_3_GTM_Pricing_BM_Scale: Pay-per-lead tiered $3-7 by premium level; future subscription $143/month unlimited
First 3 AI leads free then paid subscription or 10-15% revenue share on closed deals
Agent ACV ~$3,400/yr; LTV/CAC 15-20x via organic referrals and agent self-onboarding
Two-sided revenue from agent pay-per-lead fees plus future consumer AI design fees at ~$7 per generated plan
B2B multiplier: one GA partnership onboards hundreds of agents at once — revenue scales faster than customer count
Stage 1: Expert matching platform with manual design — position as multi-agent comparison to build supply-side network of 1,000+ agents and validate demand
Stage 2: AI Design Manager MVP — A/B test AI-designed vs agent-only proposals to prove customer trust in AI recommendations and validate willingness to pay
Stage 3: Obtain GA license and transition to AI-first distribution — AI handles 80%+ of initial design and selection while human agents refine complex cases
Pure software AI Design Manager serves unlimited customers at near-zero marginal cost versus traditional GA needing one-to-one human agent effort per customer
Two-sided network effects compound automatically — more consumers attract agents chasing leads and more agents improve options creating self-reinforcing growth
Korea to Japan ($325B, 92.9% face-to-face) to SEA — same GA bias structure enables direct model replication with identical AI stack at zero marginal cost
slide_6_Team_1_4_Composition_&_Roles: CEO Seongjae and CPO Minjun bring 20yr combined insurance expertise from Heungkuk Fire and Marine covering sales, claims, and fraud detection — invested $65K personal capital into DOPDA
CTO Sookhyun was found at Antler — ex-DayTrip AI engineer with 4yr AI/ML plus full-stack experience, CUHK CS degree, and prior co-founding experience at Pawlog (1.8yr)
CEO leads GTM and fundraising leveraging 10yr field expertise; CPO drives product vision and data analysis; CTO architects and builds the AI Design Manager platform end-to-end
Pivoted from proposal-first to chat-first model after Bootcamp feedback showing product structure was the bottleneck — executed the pivot in one week with data-driven decision-making
10yr Heungkuk Fire & Marine Insurance — Sales Branch Manager + Claims Review
Led agent teams; understands agent pain points from DB leads to manual proposals
Leads GTM strategy, fundraising, insurance operations, and external partnerships
10yr Heungkuk Fire & Marine Insurance — Insurance Fraud Data Analysis + Claims
Built data models to detect fraudulent claims; expert in insurance data structures
Drives product vision, data analysis, and statistical validation of AI results
Ex-DayTrip AI Engineer (2.5yr AI/ML) + Full-stack (4yr); CUHK CS degree
Prior co-founder at Pawlog (1.8yr) — built product from zero to launch independently
Architects AI Design Manager: recommendation engine, platform, and infrastructure
slide_7_Team_2_4_Program_Trajectory: CEO and CPO preformed from Heungkuk Insurance (10yr each, sales + claims + fraud) joined Antler with live DOPDA product and 3,300+ users seeking technical co-founder
CTO found at Antler— ex-DayTrip AI engineer with 4yr AI/ML and full-stack; joined W5 after prior team dissolved completing domain plus tech founding trio
Pivot disagreement resolved via structured data analysis; DD Survey shows all items aligned across CEO and CPO — consensus-driven data-first decision culture established
Launched as AI insurance expert matching platform; Bootcamp1 Jaehee scored 3 — key feedback: whose problem are you solving, consumer or agent? Define clearly to unlock growth
Bootcamp2 Jiho scored 6: still perceived as one of many without clear differentiation; Bootcamp3-1 Gabriel scored 5: revenue targets too low and needs to prove scale beyond niche
Bootcamp5 Gabriel scored 7: trackout approved; userflow pivot from proposal-first to chat-first got positive feedback; ClaimAI still needed for usage frequency
Post-trackout chat-first pivot drove $4.3K monthly revenue with 3x MoM growth — optimizing CAC from $20-24 toward $10 target while scaling agent activation
Summarizes the journey from program start through team formation and PMF validation, based on weekly founder retrospectives and program team observations
slide_8_Team_3_4_Evaluation: CEO+CPO 10yr preformed bond strong. CTO joined W5 — very recent, minimal bonding. Equity 37.5/37.5/25
Live product with $4.3K MoM revenue (3x growth). 3,300+ users, 500+ agents. Pivot executed in one week
BC1 (3) to BC2 (6) to BC3-1 (5) to BC5 (7) trackout. Persistent iteration. Pivoted product direction
Both CEO+CPO 10yr insurance veterans (sales + claims). 38.3% conversion proven. Deep structural insight
CTO 4yr AI/ML at DayTrip + full-stack. MVP outsourced, transitioning in-house. Bubble for pivot test
AI Design Manager pre-designs insurance before agent involvement. Replaces GA bias. Novel paradigm shift
Based on weekly founder retrospectives and feedback
slide_9_Team_4_4_Strengths_&_Improvement: Strengths
● Structural market problem verified by public data
Korea insurance remains agent-driven with FSC confirming 1.85M comparison uses — structural demand for objectivity is real
● Live product with measurable early traction
3,300+ users, 500+ agents, $4.3K monthly revenue with 3x MoM growth — running commercial platform with adoption on both sides
● AI pre-design before agent contact is novel
No competitor offers AI-designed insurance plans across 30+ insurers before agent involvement — a wedge incumbents cannot claim
Areas for Improvement
● Objectivity claim remains externally unverified
No external audit or blind review confirms AI quality — core objectivity promise rests on assertion not evidence at this stage
● Regulatory position boundary unclear
Service may be information provision or de facto solicitation — no external legal opinion confirms regulatory position is clean
● Paying cohort depth appears shallow
500 registered agents with $4.3K monthly revenue implies few actually pay — economics depend on paying depth not registration
● CTO joined very recently at Week 5
Joined after prior team dissolved with limited bonding time — long-term alignment with preformed CEO and CPO duo still unproven
● Self-GA vision may undermine independence
Becoming own GA improves economics but contradicts independence brand — core value proposition weakens as revenue model deepens
Team (4/4)
Summary
Founder Alignment & Equity Structure

● Equity is split 37.5% / 37.5% / 25% among CEO, CPO, and CTO, reflecting equal founding contributions on the business side and strong ownership for the CTO given the critical role of AI in building the core product and moat
slide_10_Investor_Assessment: Insurance market they are targeting is huge across the world
Fierce competition among startups and corporates in the space. GAs are still very strong channel for customers
Defensibility not clear due to the fact that other AI planners can easily appear and compete without too high barriers of insurance expertise
Educations and perceptions on insurance products differ largely by region and cultures, so cannot say the business is easily transferrable and scalable globally

Good expertise in the domain and technical capabilities. However, they lack creativeness in validating customers' needs and coming up with ideas for impactful features and processes. Rather Antler partner has given many ideas to try to secure upstream traffic

Overall Summary : Team needs to demonstrate large consumer traffic coming in and submitting their existing insurance policies to get AI's review for coverages. With that, GAs need to consider such traffic as more valuable, pre-qualified deals so that they can pay more for sending DMs to them, which will lead to even higher revenue stream for the team. Traction is too small and not uprising just yet
Investment Decision Rationale : Not quite sure whether the team can become a dominant player over competition that is likely to rise and get more fierce quite soon

Key Risks : Competition may rise quickly and moat is not likely to be formed quickly around LLM-leveraging solution. Not clear whether consumers will buy the solution's neutrality over insurance companies, or the best product tailored for them regardless of who the policy provider is
